data.title: Nissan to seek investment from Tesla after fallout with Honda - report
Lundin Gold Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54, revenue of $341.79M; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Lundin Gold press release (OTCQX:LUGDF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54.
  • Revenue of $341.79M (+79.2% Y/Y).
  • Record high cash flow with cash from operating activities of $662 million and adjusted free cash flow of $540 million or $2.26 per share.
  • Gold production was 135,241 oz, comprised of 88,834 oz in concentrate and 46,407 oz as doré.
  • During the fourth quarter, 405,529 tonnes of ore were mined while the mill processed 427,030 tonnes of ore at an average throughput of 4,642 tonnes per day.
  • The average ore grade milled was 11.3 grams per tonne with average recovery at 87.1%, both improvements from the third quarter of 2024.

  • Outlook

    • The company maintains its 2025 guidance with gold production estimated between 475,000 to 525,000 oz based on an average throughput rate of 5,000 tpd. Head grade is estimated to average 9.2 g/t while average mill recovery is estimated at 90%. Cash operating costs4 are estimated to range between $730 and $790 per oz of gold sold in 2025. AISC1 for 2025 is expected to range between $935 and $995 per oz of gold sold and to fluctuate quarterly based on sustaining capital activities. Unit costs are anticipated to be higher compared to 2024 and are primarily attributable to increased royalties and employee profit sharing resulting from the increase in the assumed gold price from $1,900 per oz to $2,500 per oz, an increase in power tariffs, and an increase in sustaining capital expenditures.

data.title: HanesBrands prices senior secured term Loan B facility
data.description:
HanesBrands (NYSE:HBI) Thursday said that it has priced its senior secured term loan B facility in an aggregate principal amount of $1.1 billion to replace the company’s existing $300 million Term Loan B due 2030.

The Term Loan B will mature seven years after the date of closing, which is expected to occur in the next few weeks.

The Term Loan B priced at SOFR + 275 basis points with an OID of 99 3/4.

Additionally, HanesBrands (NYSE:HBI) announced a $750 million senior secured revolving credit facility due 2030 to replace the company’s existing $1.0 billion senior secured revolving credit due 2026 and a $400M Term Loan A facility due 2030 to replace existing $403 million term loan A due 2026.

Hanesbrands intends to use the net proceeds from the Term Loan B, together with the proceeds from the Revolver and Term Loan A, to redeem the company’s outstanding 4.875% Senior Notes due 2026.

data.title: UK retail sales rise 1.7% in January
Asia markets mixed on U.S. trade tariffs and rates jitters; Alibaba fuels Hong Kong rally
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade in a tight range on Friday amid persistent concerns over U.S. trade tariffs and high for longer interest rates, while strong earnings from e-commerce giant Alibaba sparked a renewed rally in Hong Kong. Invesrors also react after U.S. President Donald Trump stated a trade deal with China was "possible", although he did not provide specific details.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 0.28% to 38,500, while the broader Topix Index fell 0.3% to 2,726 on Friday. The Japanese yen fell past 150 per dollar on Friday, retreating from 11-week highs, even as hotter-than-expected inflation figures strengthened the case for a hawkish outlook on Bank of Japan monetary policy.

Meanwhile, data showed that core inflation in Japan accelerated to 3.2% in January, up from 3% in December, surpassing forecasts of 3.1%. Headline inflation also increased to 4%, up from 3.6%, marking the highest level in two years.

Meanwhile, Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda commented on Friday that "from a long-term perspective, rising interest rates will help improve financial institutions' profits." He emphasized that the central bank is prepared to respond swiftly to market shifts, including through market operations if abnormal moves occur.

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.84% to above 3,355, and the Shenzhen Component climbing 1.3% to 10,940, marking a ten-week high. The rally was fueled by US President Donald Trump's comments suggesting that a new trade deal with China was possible, signaling a more positive outlook for US-China relations. The offshore yuan weakened toward 7.24 per dollar, despite the People’s Bank of China pledging to bolster the currency amidst mounting pressure from the stronger US dollar.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 3.29% to 23,115 in early deals on Friday, reversing losses in the prior session amid robust gains in most sectors notably consumers and tech. Alibaba Group Hlds. surged over 9%, hitting its highest in over 3 years after reporting the strongest revenue growth in more than a year.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.48% to 75,247, touching its lowest level since early June 2024 in morning trade on Friday, falling for the third straight session, with almost all sectors moving in the red, led by auto and pharmaceuticals.

The HSBC India Manufacturing PMI fell to 57.1 in February 2025, down from 57.7 in the previous month, according to preliminary estimates. The HSBC India Services PMI increased to 61.1 in February 2025, up from 56.5 in the previous month, which was the softest pace since November 2022, preliminary estimates showed. The HSBC India Composite PMI increased to 60.6 in February 2025 from 57.7 in the previous month, flash data showed.

Market sentiment was downbeat, with rising concerns over potential US tariffs on Indian goods leading to capital outflows.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.32% to close at 8,296 on Friday and declined more than 3% over the week, driven by a selloff in heavyweight iron ore miners and bank stocks following disappointing earnings reports. Sentiment was also dampened by the Reserve Bank of Australia’s hawkish stance on interest rates, signaling a more gradual easing path than previously expected. The Australian dollar rose to around $0.64 on Friday, reaching its highest level in ten weeks as strong economic data fueled expectations of a more gradual easing cycle from the Reserve Bank of Australia.

Data showed that private sector growth in Australia extended into a fifth consecutive month in February, with both manufacturing and services activities remaining in expansionary territory. The S&P Global Australia Manufacturing PMI rose to 50.6 in February 2025 from 50.2 in January, signaling the second consecutive expansion in over two years.

Additionally, recent reports indicated that employment rose more than expected in December, pointing to a robust labor market.

In the U.S., on Thursday, all three major indexes ended lower after Walmart’s disappointing outlook fueled concerns about economic growth.

Meanwhile, minutes from the latest FOMC meeting reaffirmed the Fed’s cautious stance, signaling no immediate urgency to ease policy amid an uncertain economic outlook.

U.S. stock futures steadied on Friday following a selloff in the previous session, triggered by Walmart’s disappointing outlook, which raised concerns about economic growth: Dow +0.03%; S&P 500 -0.02%; Nasdaq +0.02%.

Traders are now focused on Friday’s PMI readings and existing home sales data for additional insights into the economy.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan's CPI surpass expectations; annual inflation hits 2-year high to 4%

Japan reports larger-than-expected trade deficit for Jan, imports outpaces exports

Trump says 25% tariffs targeting cars, chips & pharma imports coming as soon as April 2

RBA cuts its cash rate by 25 bps for first time since 2020, in-line with market expectations

Japan’s economy expands by 0.7% Q/Q in Q4, beats estimates

data.title: Metavista3D announces private placement financing
data.description:
• Metavista3D (TSXV:DDD:CA) Friday said that it intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of up to 1,436,781 common shares in the capital of the company at a price per common share of $1.74 for gross proceeds of $2.5 million.
  • The proceeds raised from the offering will be used for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Japan's CPI surpass expectations; annual inflation hits 2-year high to 4%
data.description:
Data showed core inflation in Japan rose to 3.2% in January, up from 3% in December, exceeding forecasts of 3.1%. Headline inflation also increased to 4%, up from 3.6%, marking the highest level in two years.

The January figure also represents the highest level since June 2023, further solidifying a hawkish outlook for the Bank of Japan's monetary policy.

The core inflation rate rose to a 19-month high of 3.2%, up from 3.0% in December and topping consensus of 3.1%.

Monthly, the CPI increased by 0.5%, after December's 14-month top of 0.6% rise.

Food prices in Japan increased by 7.8% from a year earlier in January 2025, accelerating from a 6.4% rise in the previous month. It was the highest food inflation since October 2023, with upward price pressures.

Separately, the Au Jibun Bank Japan Manufacturing PMI inched higher to 48.9 in February 2025 from January’s ten-month low of 48.7, compared to market estimates of 49, preliminary estimates showed. The au Jibun Bank Japan Services PMI stood at 53.1 in January 2025, marginally up from 53.0 the previous month, indicating the highest level since last September, a preliminary reading showed. The au Jibun Bank Japan Composite PMI rose to 51.6 in January 2025, up from 51.1 the previous month, flash data showed.

Meanwhile, Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda commented on Friday that "from a long-term perspective, rising interest rates will help improve financial institutions' profits." He emphasized that the central bank is prepared to respond swiftly to market shifts, including through market operations if abnormal moves occur.

The Nikkei 225 Index dropped 0.5% to below 38,500, while the broader Topix Index fell 0.3% to 2,726 on Friday, marking a third consecutive session of declines. The Japanese yen fell past 150 per dollar on Friday, retreating from 11-week highs, even as hotter-than-expected inflation figures strengthened the case for a hawkish outlook on Bank of Japan monetary policy.

More on Japan

  • Japan’s core inflation rate surges to 16-month high of 3% in December

  • Bank of Japan raises policy rate to 0.5%, highest since 2008 amid sustained inflation and rising wages

  • Japan unexpectedly logs trade surplus, exports hits record high & imports also shows positive growth

  • BOJ's Dec summary raises expectations of rate hike soon; keeps policy rate steady at 0.25%

  • Japan’s Nov headline inflation rate rises to three-month high of 2.9%; core inflation tops forecast

Solaris Oilfield Infrastructure reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and Q2 Adjusted EBITDA outlook
data.description:
• Solaris Oilfield Infrastructure press release (NYSE:SEI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.12 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $96M (+51.7% Y/Y) beats by $2.03M.
  • Total Adjusted EBITDA of $37 million.
  • Net cash from operating activities was $13 million in the fourth quarter 2024, and capital expenditures were approximately $127 million.
  • The company expects first quarter 2025 Total Adjusted EBITDA to be between $44 and $48 million and second quarter Total Adjusted EBITDA to be between $50 and $55 million.
